title: `podman container prune --filter until=` | All stopped containers removed regardless the time value they lastly were created within
body:
### Issue Description

### For reference
Manual _podman-container-prune(1)_
```
 Remove all stopped containers from local storage created within last 10 minutes.
  $ podman container prune --filter until="10m"
```
### Description
Hello. The `until` filter removes all stopped containers from local storage regardless the time value they lastly were created within.
```
$ podman ps -a
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE                           COMMAND           CREATED         STATUS                    PORTS                 NAMES
0efb80fe2e82  docker.io/library/httpd:latest  httpd-foreground  22 seconds ago  Exited (0) 5 seconds ago  0.0.0.0:8080->80/tcp  elastic_swanson
$ podman run -dt -p 8080:80/tcp docker.io/library/httpd:latest
0efb80fe2e829bce07cbb19bd61da807f6fce2da3ac43d59901c394667d9acfc
```

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Shortest time value chosen, _one second_, so that no container is expected to be eligible for selection for removal as **no container was lastly created within that value**:
```
$ podman container prune --filter until="1s"
WARNING! This will remove all non running containers.
Are you sure you want to continue? [y/N] y
0efb80fe2e829bce07cbb19bd61da807f6fce2da3ac43d59901c394667d9acfc
```

### Describe the results you received

All non running containers that were non eligible for removal were removed from local storage.

### Describe the results you expected

Output's presented in this order

1. **Exhaustive description** of the future operation; model:

`[Warming] This will remove all non running containers created within the last 1 second(s) from local storage.`

2. Previous statement followed by the selection of the eligible containers.

3. The presentation of choice:

"`Are you sure you want to continue? [No/yes]`".

Since no non running container was created within the **last second**, there should have been no container eligible for removal.

### podman info output

comments.body:
Thanks for reaching out, @Ricky-Tigg!

@jakecorrenti, interested in taking a look at it?
Sure thing, I'll take a look

An investigation to determine the time/duration formats led me to https://pkg.go.dev/time#Duration.

"_A duration string is a possibly signed sequence of decimal numbers, each with optional fraction and a unit suffix, such as "300ms", "-1.5h" or "2h45m". Valid time units are "ns", "us" (or "µs"), "ms", "s", "m", "h"._"

Hence, supposing that source as being correct, Podman behaves as intended by failing to parse units matching _day_, _month_ and _year_, respectively possibly, _d_ or _D_, _M_ and _y_ or _Y_ that are as time value in `podman` commands.

Hi @Ricky-Tigg, thank you for filing this issue. The behavior that you are experiencing with the `--filter until` flag is correct and will remove all containers **except** those created within the time specified. 

If you are trying to remove all containers that were created within the last 10 minutes, you are looking for a `since` filter, which I filed an issue for in #19228

The documentation's example you noted was incorrect and will be changed in #19227.
